 {"id":"bd-2qcn","title":"Improve text truncation to preserve useful information","description":"## Problem
Text truncation is too aggressive, cutting off useful information and showing many entries that look identical.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Confirmed: Simple End Truncation

**layout.go:71-83** - TruncateRunes just cuts from end:
```go
func TruncateRunes(s string, max int, suffix string) string {
    runes := []rune(s)
    if len(runes) <= max {
        return s
    }
    return string(runes[:max-len([]rune(suffix))]) + suffix  // Always cuts end
}
```

This produces:
```
destructive_command_guar...
destructive_command_guar...  // Indistinguishable!
```

When the unique part is:
```
destructive_command_guard_cc_1
destructive_command_guard_cc_2
```

## Fix Implementation

### 1. Smart Truncation (Middle Ellipsis)
```go
// TruncateMiddle keeps beginning and end, ellipsis in middle
func TruncateMiddle(s string, max int) string {
    if max <= 0 {
        return ""
    }
    runes := []rune(s)
    if len(runes) <= max {
        return s
    }

    ellipsis := "…"
    ellipsisLen := 1
    available := max - ellipsisLen

    // Keep more from the end (unique part) than beginning
    endChars := available * 2 / 3
    startChars := available - endChars

    if startChars < 3 {
        startChars = 3
    }
    if endChars < 3 {
        endChars = 3
    }

    return string(runes[:startChars]) + ellipsis + string(runes[len(runes)-endChars:])
}

// Result: "destru…_cc_16"
```

### 2. Common Prefix Detection
```go
// TruncateWithCommonPrefix for lists of similar strings
func TruncateWithCommonPrefix(items []string, maxWidth int) []string {
    if len(items) <= 1 {
        return truncateAll(items, maxWidth)
    }

    // Find common prefix
    prefix := items[0]
    for _, item := range items[1:] {
        prefix = commonPrefix(prefix, item)
    }

    // If significant common prefix, show abbreviated
    if len(prefix) > maxWidth/2 {
        // Show: "[prefix]…_cc_1" -> "[dcg]…_cc_1"
        abbrev := abbreviate(prefix)
        // ...
    }
    return truncateAll(items, maxWidth)
}
```

### 3. Numbered Suffix
```go
// For lists with common names, add distinguishing numbers
func renderListWithNumbers(items []string, maxWidth int) []string {
    counts := make(map[string]int)
    result := make([]string, len(items))

    for i, item := range items {
        truncated := TruncateMiddle(item, maxWidth-6) // Leave room for " (1/N)"
        counts[truncated]++
        if counts[truncated] > 1 {
            result[i] = truncated + fmt.Sprintf(" (%d)", counts[truncated])
        } else {
            result[i] = truncated
        }
    }
    return result
}
```

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestTruncateMiddle(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        input string
        max   int
        want  string
    }{
        {"destructive_command_guard_cc_16", 20, "destru…d_cc_16"},
        {"short", 20, "short"},
        {"abcdefghij", 7, "abc…ij"},
    }

    for _, tc := range tests {
        got := TruncateMiddle(tc.input, tc.max)
        if got != tc.want {
            t.Errorf("TruncateMiddle(%q, %d) = %q, want %q",
                tc.input, tc.max, got, tc.want)
        }
        if lipgloss.Width(got) > tc.max {
            t.Errorf("result width %d exceeds max %d",
                lipgloss.Width(got), tc.max)
        }
    }
}

func TestSimilarItemsDistinguishable(t *testing.T) {
    items := []string{
        "destructive_command_guard_cc_1",
        "destructive_command_guard_cc_2",
        "destructive_command_guard_cc_16",
    }

    truncated := truncateAll(items, 25)

    // All truncated items should be unique
    seen := make(map[string]bool)
    for _, item := range truncated {
        if seen[item] {
            t.Errorf("duplicate truncated item: %q", item)
        }
        seen[item] = true
    }
}
```

## E2E Test (VHS)
```tape
# truncation_test.tape
Set Width 120
Type "ntm dashboard --demo-data"
Sleep 2s

# With many similarly named items, they should be distinguishable
Screenshot truncation_distinguishable.png

Set Width 80
Sleep 1s
Screenshot truncation_narrow.png
```

## Verification
- [ ] TruncateMiddle preserves unique suffix
- [ ] Similar entries are visually distinguishable
- [ ] Panel width adjusts for content when possible
- [ ] Works with emoji and CJK in names","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:09:24.134632524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:57.541500167Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:57.541445244Z","close_reason":"Implemented TruncateMiddle and TruncateMiddleWidth functions in internal/tui/layout/layout.go. Added comprehensive unit tests. Also fixed broken terminal package as part of this work.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qcn","depends_on_id":"bd-1ay2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qcn","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qdhp","title":"CRITICAL: Always send Enter twice after marching orders","description":"When sending marching orders to agents via tmux send-keys, Enter must be sent TWICE.

## The Problem
Agents appear to receive marching orders but never start processing them.

## Root Cause
A single Enter key press may not reliably submit the message in Claude Code / Codex CLI.
The message sits in the input buffer without being submitted.

## The Fix
After sending the marching orders text, ALWAYS send Enter twice with a small delay:

```bash
# Send marching orders (no Enter at end)
tmux send-keys -t "$session:1.$pane" "$msg"
sleep 1
# CRITICAL: Send Enter TWICE
tmux send-keys -t "$session:1.$pane" Enter
sleep 0.5
tmux send-keys -t "$session:1.$pane" Enter
```

## Code Locations to Fix
- internal/commands/spawn.go - sendMarchingOrders()
- internal/commands/send.go - sendToPane()
- Any function that sends text to agent panes

## Testing
After fix, verify with:
```bash
tmux capture-pane -t session:1.1 -p | tail -20
```
Should show agent actively processing, not waiting at input prompt.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:31:04.196222340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:14:57.484887937Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:14:57.484842011Z","close_reason":"Send prompts with double Enter and delays across CLI agent sends","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2qfa5","title":"Task: Define ReasoningMode struct and related types in internal/ensemble/types.go","description":"# Task: Define ReasoningMode struct and related types

**STATUS: PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED**

The following types are now implemented in internal/ensemble/types.go:

## Implemented Types

### Core Mode Types
- ReasoningMode - Mode definition with ID, Name, Category, etc.
- ReasoningCategory - Category enum (Formal, Ampliative, etc.)
- ModeCatalog - Indexed catalog with lookup methods

### Output Schema Types (NEW - Critical for mechanical merging)
- ImpactLevel - high/medium/low enum with validation
- Confidence - 0.0-1.0 score with validation
- Finding - Discovery with impact, confidence, evidence_pointer
- Risk - Potential problem with severity, likelihood
- Recommendation - Suggested action with priority, effort
- Question - Unresolved query for user
- FailureModeWarning - Reasoning pitfall warning
- ModeOutput - Complete output schema (mandatory for all modes)

### Configuration Types
- BudgetConfig - Token limits, timeouts, retries
- SynthesisConfig - Strategy, min confidence, max findings
- Ensemble - Curated mode collection (primary user interface)
- SynthesisStrategy - Enum (consensus, adversarial, etc.)

## Remaining Work
- None - types are complete

## Test Coverage
- types_test.go includes comprehensive validation tests
- All Validate() methods tested
- Default config functions tested

 {"id":"bd-2qwm8","title":"Feature: Synthesis System - Combining multi-mode outputs","description":"# Feature: Synthesis System - Stage 3 of pipeline

**Two-path synthesis architecture:**
- **Agent-based**: Synthesizer + Disagreement Auditor
- **Manual/Mechanical**: No synthesizer agent; deterministic merge + auditor only

## Stage 3 Components

### 1. Output Collector
- Validate each output against OutputSchema
- Flag non-compliant outputs
- Aggregate into SynthesisInput

### 2. Synthesizer Agent (when strategy requires agent)
- Merge outputs into cohesive report
- Rank findings by impact × confidence
- Generate executive summary

### 3. Disagreement Auditor
- Identify contradictory theses and risks
- Surface conflicts and resolution paths

### 4. Output Formatter
- Markdown/JSON/YAML export of SynthesisResult
- Deterministic ordering

## Strategy Support
- Manual strategies should **skip synthesizer agent** but still run auditor + formatter.
- Agent strategies use templates from bd-3k4sh and definitions from bd-2ztw9.

## Files
- internal/ensemble/collector.go
- internal/ensemble/synthesizer.go
- internal/ensemble/auditor.go
- internal/ensemble/merge.go
- internal/ensemble/formatter.go","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"claude-opus-4.5","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:26:02.694077251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:56:29.175540344Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:56:29.175514615Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qwm8","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qwm8","depends_on_id":"bd-2ouwm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qwm8","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-zcft","title":"Task: Create Ollama adapter for local LLM communication","description":"## Overview
Implement an adapter that connects NTM to local Ollama instances for running local LLMs.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/agent/ollama/adapter.go:
   - OllamaAdapter struct implementing AgentBackend interface
   - Connect(host string) error
   - SendPrompt(prompt string) (Response, error)
   - StreamResponse(prompt string) (<-chan Token, error)
   - ListModels() ([]Model, error)
   - PullModel(name string) error

2. Ollama API integration:
   - POST /api/generate for completions
   - POST /api/chat for chat format
   - GET /api/tags for model listing
   - POST /api/pull for model download

3. Response handling:
   - Parse streaming JSON responses
   - Extract token content, completion status
   - Track token generation rate

4. Error handling:
   - Connection errors (Ollama not running)
   - Model not found (suggest pull)
   - Context length exceeded
   - GPU memory exhausted

## Implementation Notes
- Use net/http for API calls
- Handle NDJSON streaming
- Support custom Ollama host via NTM_OLLAMA_HOST

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can connect to local Ollama instance
- Prompts sent and responses received
- Streaming works for real-time output
- Errors handled gracefully with helpful messages
- Unit tests with mock Ollama server","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:29:39.666892901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:06:26.618222933Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:06:26.616414988Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zcft","depends_on_id":"bd-1t8p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-00u","title":"Implement rate limit backoff for agents","description":"Detect rate limit errors and notify users (do not auto-retry).

## What This Task Does

Detect when agents hit rate limits and notify users. This is simpler
and safer than auto-retry, which can cause more problems than it solves.

## Why Detection Only (No Auto-Retry)

Auto-retry is complex and error-prone:
- Can't reliably capture the 'last command' to replay
- Agent state may be corrupted after rate limit
- Infinite retry loops are possible
- Users should decide if/when to retry

Instead: Detect, notify, let user handle it.

## Detection

Rate limit patterns in agent output:
- 'rate limit exceeded'
- 'too many requests'
- '429'
- 'quota exceeded'
- 'try again in X seconds'

## Response

When rate limit detected:
1. Mark agent as 'rate_limited' in health status
2. Fire notification event (desktop, webhook, shell)
3. Include wait time if parseable from error
4. Show in 'ntm health' output with clear indicator

Example notification:
'Agent cc_1 hit rate limit. Suggested wait: 60s'

## Integration

- Uses health check infrastructure (ntm-239) for detection
- Uses notification system (ntm-gcv) for alerts
- Shows in 'ntm status' with rate_limited indicator

## Configuration

[resilience.rate_limit]
detect = true
notify = true
patterns = ['rate limit', '429', 'too many requests']

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Rate limit patterns detected in pane output
- [ ] Notification fired when rate limit detected
- [ ] Wait time parsed when available
- [ ] Status shown in ntm health output
- [ ] No auto-retry (by design)
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:48:15.213867Z","updated_at":"2025-12-10T15:20:28.043769Z","closed_at":"2025-12-10T15:20:28.043769Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-00u","depends_on_id":"ntm-239","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-014q","title":"Feature: Policy Engine","description":"# Policy Engine (internal/policy/)

## Why This Is Critical
The .ntm/policy.yaml file is referenced by MULTIPLE features but there's no dedicated engine to parse and enforce it:
- Destructive Command Protection (blocked/allowed patterns)
- BD Daemon Mode (auto-push policy gate)
- File Reservation Lifecycle (force-release approval)
- SLB Safety Gates (approval_required operations)

Without a unified Policy Engine, each feature reimplements policy checking, leading to inconsistencies and bugs.

## Policy File Structure
```yaml
# .ntm/policy.yaml
version: 1

# Commands that are always blocked
blocked:
  - pattern: "git reset --hard"
    reason: "Hard reset loses commits"
  - pattern: "git checkout --"
    reason: "Discards uncommitted changes"

# Commands that require approval
approval_required:
  - pattern: "git push --force"
    reason: "Force push rewrites history"
    slb: true  # Requires SLB two-person rule
  - pattern: "force_release"
    reason: "Releases another agent's reservation"
    slb: false  # Just needs any approval

# Commands that bypass checks
allowed:
  - pattern: "git checkout -b"
    reason: "Create branch is safe"

# Automation settings
automation:
  auto_push: false
  auto_commit: true
  force_release: approval
```

## Implementation (internal/policy/engine.go)

### Policy Engine Interface
```go
type PolicyEngine struct {
    policyPath string
    policy     *Policy
    mu         sync.RWMutex
}

type Policy struct {
    Version          int              `yaml:"version"`
    Blocked          []PatternRule    `yaml:"blocked"`
    ApprovalRequired []PatternRule    `yaml:"approval_required"`
    Allowed          []PatternRule    `yaml:"allowed"`
    Automation       AutomationConfig `yaml:"automation"`
}

type PatternRule struct {
    Pattern    string   `yaml:"pattern"`
    Reason     string   `yaml:"reason"`
    SLB        bool     `yaml:"slb,omitempty"`
    Exceptions []string `yaml:"exceptions,omitempty"`
}
```

### Key Methods
```go
func (e *PolicyEngine) Load(path string) error
func (e *PolicyEngine) Reload() error  // Watch for file changes
func (e *PolicyEngine) IsBlocked(command string) (bool, string)
func (e *PolicyEngine) NeedsApproval(action string) (bool, *PatternRule)
func (e *PolicyEngine) IsAllowed(command string) bool
func (e *PolicyEngine) AutomationEnabled(feature string) bool
```

### File Watching
Policy file is watched for changes and automatically reloaded:
```go
func (e *PolicyEngine) StartWatcher(ctx context.Context) {
    watcher, _ := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
    watcher.Add(e.policyPath)
    // Reload on change
}
```

### Default Policy
If .ntm/policy.yaml doesn't exist, use sensible defaults (block dangerous patterns, require approval for force operations).

## Integration Points
- Destructive Command Protection: Uses IsBlocked(), IsAllowed()
- BD Daemon: Uses AutomationEnabled("auto_push")
- File Reservation: Uses NeedsApproval("force_release")
- SLB: Uses NeedsApproval() with SLB flag check

## New Commands
```bash
ntm policy show           # Display current policy
ntm policy validate       # Check policy file syntax
ntm policy reset          # Reset to defaults
ntm policy edit           # Open in editor
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Policy file parsed correctly
- [ ] Default policy used when file missing
- [ ] File watching with auto-reload
- [ ] IsBlocked/NeedsApproval/IsAllowed work
- [ ] AutomationEnabled checks work
- [ ] Validation command catches syntax errors","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-04T01:27:17.437246Z","updated_at":"2026-01-04T04:15:59.329965Z","closed_at":"2026-01-04T04:15:59.329965Z","close_reason":"Policy engine complete with: Policy struct (Version, Blocked, ApprovalRequired, Allowed, Automation), AutomationConfig (auto_commit, auto_push, force_release), SLB support, CLI commands (show, validate, reset, edit, automation), comprehensive tests.  {"id":"ntm-03ae","title":"Integrate routing with Agent Mail file reservations","description":"When routing work, consider Agent Mail file reservations as a signal for task affinity.

RATIONALE:
If an agent has reserved files via Agent Mail, they're likely the best agent to handle work related to those files. This creates natural task affinity.

INTEGRATION POINTS:

1. RESERVATION QUERY:
   - Query Agent Mail for active reservations in project
   - Cache results (refresh every 30s or on routing request)
   - Map: pane_id -> []reserved_paths

2. AFFINITY SCORING:
   - Extract file paths from prompt (patterns like "src/auth.go", "internal/")
   - Check if any agent has matching reservations
   - Add affinity bonus (configurable, default 30) to matching agent

3. CONFLICT PREVENTION:
   - If prompt mentions file reserved by another agent:
     - Warning in response: "File reserved by {agent}"
     - Option: --respect-reservations to skip reserved files

CONFIGURATION:
```toml
[routing.agent_mail]
enabled = true
reservation_bonus = 30
respect_reservations = false  # warn only vs enforce
cache_ttl = "30s"
```

DEPENDENCY:
- Requires Agent Mail MCP to be available
- Graceful degradation if unavailable

LOCATION: internal/robot/routing.go (extend existing)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- File reservations queried from Agent Mail
- Matching reservations boost agent score
- Warning when sending to non-reserving agent
- Works gracefully without Agent Mail","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-31T02:43:46.966240335Z","updated_at":"2026-01-09T20:27:14.261923918Z","closed_at":"2026-01-06T03:28:15.325666Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-03ae","depends_on_id":"ntm-m961","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
